name: Advance America
description_short: Advance America offers payday loans ($100–$500), installment loans ($100–$1,000), and title loans ($2,000–$25,000) with same-day funding at physical locations and online.
description_long: Advance America is a nationally recognized, fully accredited lender that operates physical store locations across the United States, including the Jacksonville, FL branch at 1020-14 N. Edgewood Ave. The company has built its business around providing fast, short-term cash solutions to consumers facing immediate financial needs between paychecks or unexpected expenses. With over 125,448 customer reviews and a 4.9-star rating on Google, the company positions itself as a trusted provider in the emergency lending space. Their Jacksonville location operates Monday–Thursday 10 a.m.–6 p.m., Friday 9 a.m.–6 p.m., and Saturday 9 a.m.–4 p.m., with Spanish-language support available. Advance America offers three primary loan products: Payday Loans for short-term cash needs (2–4 weeks), Installment Loans for larger amounts with extended repayment terms, and Title Loans that leverage vehicle equity for amounts up to $25,000. The company emphasizes accessibility—loans can be obtained online or in-store with minimal documentation (government ID, proof of income, checking account, and SSN/ITIN). They also offer Western Union money transfer services at their locations. What distinguishes Advance America is its multi-product approach, allowing consumers to choose loan structures based on their specific cash needs and repayment capacity. The company advertises rapid approval and funding timelines, with same-day personal loans up to $1,000 prominently featured. Their customer service model emphasizes in-store guidance, with staff trained to help borrowers select appropriate loan products. However, consumers should recognize that payday and title loans typically carry high APRs—a fact notably absent from the company's marketing materials. The loan amounts are inherently small ($100–$500 for payday loans), making them suitable only for immediate cash gaps, not broader financial needs. While installment loans offer longer repayment windows and higher caps ($1,000), they remain primarily emergency products rather than conventional personal loans. Borrowers should carefully evaluate their repayment capacity before committing, as the cost of these loans can be substantial over time.
address: 1221 B E Valley Pkwy, Escondido, CA 92027

company_info.headquarters: 1221 B E Valley Pkwy, Escondido, CA 92027

pros: Same-day funding available for payday and installment loans up to $1,000
Multiple loan products (payday, installment, title loans) allowing borrowers to choose repayment structures
High customer satisfaction with 4.9/5 stars based on 125,448 reviews
Convenient in-store locations with extended hours (open until 6 p.m. weekdays) plus online application option
Spanish-language support available at physical locations
Title loans offer access to larger amounts ($2,000–$25,000) for vehicle owners
Additional services like Western Union money transfers available at store locations
cons: APR rates not disclosed on website—payday and title loans typically carry very high interest rates (often 300–500% APR)
Payday loans have short repayment windows (2–4 weeks), creating risk of debt cycles if borrower cannot repay on schedule
Title loans require vehicle as collateral, risking vehicle repossession if borrower defaults
Installment loans capped at $1,000, limiting utility for consumers with larger emergency expenses
No transparent pricing or rate calculator provided on website to help borrowers estimate actual loan costs before commitment
best_for: Consumers with immediate cash needs ($100–$500) between paychecks who can repay within 2–4 weeks
Vehicle owners with short-term liquidity needs who can access title loans ($2,000–$25,000)
Borrowers who prefer in-person service and guidance over entirely online lending platforms
Spanish-speaking consumers in Jacksonville area seeking accessible emergency lending
diagnosis: Advance America is best for consumers facing immediate cash emergencies who can repay quickly and have access to a physical store or online application. The primary caveat is that payday and title loans carry very high APRs not disclosed on the website—borrowers must understand the full cost of borrowing before committing and should exhaust alternatives (employer advances, credit union PALs, credit cards) first.
services: Installment loans online or in-store ($100–$1,000) with multiple payment terms
Title loans online ($2,000–$25,000) using vehicle equity as collateral
In-store loan applications with staff guidance
Phone-based pre-qualification over the phone at (904) 781-7075

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
